title: PayPal vs Crypto for How to Build a Crypto Payment Store for Digital Products — An Honest Comparison
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Getting paid for your digital work should not depend on which country you were born in. Here is how creators everywhere collect real income.

content.text: Having a great digital product and being unable to collect payment is a frustrating experience many creators face, especially those in restricted countries where traditional payment platforms like PayPal and Stripe are not available or have strict requirements, leaving them without a reliable way to receive international payments and forcing them to look for alternative solutions, such as crypto payment stores, that can help them reach a global audience and get paid without the hassle of traditional banking systems.
What You Are Actually Comparing

When comparing PayPal to crypto for building a payment store, it's essential to understand that you're not just comparing two payment methods, but two entirely different systems, one that's centralized and controlled by a single entity, and the other that's decentralized and open to anyone, with different rules, fees, and levels of accessibility, and creators need to consider these differences when deciding which one to use for their digital products, taking into account factors such as ease of use, security, and global reach.
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Traditional platforms like PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip often break down for creators in restricted countries due to strict KYC requirements, hold periods, and withdrawal limits, which can delay payments for weeks or even months, and in some cases, even freeze accounts without warning, leaving creators without access to their funds and unable to conduct business, and this is where crypto-native infrastructure comes in, offering a more reliable and efficient way to receive payments and conduct transactions.
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure, such as Unchained Commerce's Core Shop Engine and Web3 Wallet Connect, does things differently by providing a decentralized and automated way to receive payments, without the need for traditional banking systems or intermediaries, and with instant confirmation and no hold periods, creators can receive payments in a matter of minutes, and with no withdrawal limits, they can access their funds immediately, and this is a game-changer for creators in restricted countries who have been locked out of traditional payment systems.

Creators in restricted countries often have to deal with strict KYC requirements and hold periods that can delay payments for weeks or even months, which is why crypto-native infrastructure is a game-changer for them.
The Real Cost Difference

The real cost difference between traditional platforms and crypto-native infrastructure lies not just in the fees, but in the time and hassle saved, as creators no longer have to deal with hold periods, withdrawal limits, and account freezes, and can focus on what they do best, creating and selling digital products, and with crypto-native infrastructure, creators can also reach a global audience and receive payments in a variety of cryptocurrencies, such as USDT, BTC, and ETH, without the need for traditional banking systems.

For creators in restricted countries, crypto-native infrastructure is often the better choice, as it provides a more reliable and efficient way to receive payments and conduct transactions, and with the right tools, such as Unchained Commerce's Admin Dashboard, creators can also track their sales and analytics in real-time, and make data-driven decisions to grow their business, and while traditional platforms may have their advantages, for creators who need a reliable and global payment solution, crypto-native infrastructure is the way to go.
